channel.title: Comments for page "Giving Up"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Giving Up"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh no, please no, don't do this to me dude, oh please STOP.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's nothing quite like the approval of the original author. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The pocket dimension and the permanent springtime. Two things I don't mention in the article yet I imagined myself (read: stole from a demon door from Fable 2).

This is almost exactly what I'd had in mind when brainstorming. Nice and emotional. Really good stuff here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad the poem turned out OK - usually my attempts at the lyric word fail horribly.

This bit:

And things we may keep in our forever alone

Has a double meaning that becomes apparent throughout the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I immediately thought of this upon seeing the title: https://youtu.be/wcW2M65o-lg

The story itself was actually quite good, and I enjoyed it every step along the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of the rare pieces that actually manage to successfully evoke an emotional reaction out of me, and I applaud you for that. Your usage of wording and the poem alongside the excerpts from Howard's note assisted the mood of the article.

You have earned my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Far better than the original SCP. It's the damn fools fault that he tried to be so vague though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
;-;
Aw.
Here comes crushing realism once again.

Take your upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it. So many good lines here, and it feels like a perfect companion to 3881.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
So, my last attempt at an article was criticized for being too ambitious and not having enough meat on the bones to backup said ambition. I'm going for something much more simple and familiar here, based on SCP-3881, a wonderful little piece that deserves some expansion.

Sometimes there isn't a happily ever after. Sometimes life gets in the way, and true love takes a backseat.

Sometimes people give up.
